channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Synchronized Dragon Ball FighterZ Moment Goes Viral as FGC Legends Go in Different Directions
item.content:encoded:
A moment between two legendary competitors at the Dragon Ball FighterZ Masters Showdown has gone viral after a dazzling synchronized opening to their contest.

The exhibition tournament was part of Dragon Ball Games Battle Hour 2026, which celebrated several Dragon Ball-related properties, including card and fighting games.

SonicFox and Go1 play out mirrored sequence

Fighting Games Community legends, Dominique ‘SonicFox’ McLean and Goichi ‘Go1’ Kishida met each other in the opening round of the Dragon Ball FighterZ Masters Showdown, a matchup both players celebrated on social media prior to the contest.

As the contest began, the players had a surprise for the audience, playing out a completely synchronized and symmetrical set of inputs to perfectly parry each other’s attacks, creating a spectacle for the fans.

The moment spread like wildfire on social media, with fans noting that the sequence perfectly emulated the feel of the Dragon Ball Z anime.

However, as Professor Farnsworth once said in Futurama, “Perfectly symmetrical violence never solved anything,” so after the choreographed sequence, the players resumed normal play.

Go1 would go on to win the match comfortably with a 2-0 record, on his way to progressing to the semi-finals, where Go1 would be eliminated by eventual winner Jo’siah ‘Hikari’ Miller.

SonicFox ended the event with a 0-3 record, a result SonicFox shared was expected given his long time away from competition in the title. The player stated, “I’m glad I was able to hit the intro Sequence with Go1! Everything else was just to press buttons for fun!”

The two had planned the moves in advance, knowing it was likely the last time the two would play Dragon Ball FighterZ together. Both competitors are stepping away from the title to focus on their respective specialities.

While the competitors enjoyed a friendly rivalry in the game, contesting the EVO 2018 and 2019 Grand Finals in FighterZ, GO1’s career has shifted to focus more on Fatal Fury: City of the Wolves and Street Fighter 6, whereas SonicFox has moved towards Mortal Kombat.

Therefore, the moment was a special parting for the two legends of the Fighting Games community, sharing a laugh and a hug after the match was completed, with SonixFox describing that the players “wanted to leave the fans with one final special moment between us.”

They certainly did.

Did you appreciate the moment? You can discuss all things FGC on our Discord!
For more news, see Wolfey’s thoughts on Pokémon Champions limited roster.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/synchronized-dragon-ball-fighterz-moment-goes-viral/

item.title: Every Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Leak, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced is perhaps the worst-kept secret in gaming. Everybody and their dog knew Ubisoft were working on a remake for the titular Assassin’s Creed title, and after months of leaks and speculation, the game is finally on its way.

Insider Gaming has released a ton of leaks regarding the upcoming remake, and there’s been a lot to unpack. From the release date to the collector’s edition, and a variety of gameplay details in between, it might have been tough to keep up with the flow of news.

Luckily for you, this here is your one-stop shop for everything Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced. Every leak has been summarised below, so you can stay up to date with Edward Kenway’s return.

Every Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Leak

Insider Gaming has covered the upcoming Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced reveal in extensive detail, and there’s been a lot of leaks to keep track of. Here’s your hub for everything you need to know.

When Is Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Releasing?

Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced release date was revealed by Insider Gaming’s own Tom Henderson, and it’s ready to set sail this Summer.

  • More Insider Gaming: All Assassin’s Creed Games in Chronological Order

For the full release date and breakdown, check out the original article here.

Will Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Include Multiplayer Or DLC?

It’s a disappointing answer for many, as Insider Gaming revealed that Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced will be missing some original content.

To see what’s missing from the remake, check out the original article here.

Will Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Feature RPG Combat?

Assassin’s Creed titles have veered towards RPG combat systems in recent history, though Black Flag featured a more action-oriented experience.

To find out whether Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced will retain its original combat, check out the original article here.

Will Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Rework Animus?

The Animus has been confirmed to return in Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced, though it’s facing some notable changes.

Ubisoft are aiming to modernise the experience, and to find out how, check out the original article here.

Will Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Still Feature Tailing Missions?

Perhaps one of the weaker aspects of the original Black Flag’s gameplay was the tailing missions, and it’s been confirmed that they’ll be returning in the remake.

  • More Insider Gaming: Ubisoft Cancels Alterra, Its Animal Crossing-Inspired Game

However, Ubisoft has confirmed that they’ve been revamped; to find out how, check out the original article here.

Will Matt Ryan Return To Voice Edward Kenway?

It was disappointing to hear that Yuri Lowenthal wouldn’t return to voice the Prince in the now-cancelled Prince of Persia remake, but it’s a better turn of events for Matt Ryan.

To find out whether Matt Ryan will be returning to voice Edward Kenway, check out the original article here.

Will Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Have A Collector’s Edition?

Ubisoft titles are no strangers to collector’s editions, and it’s been confirmed that Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced will receive its very own upon release.

To find out what will be included in the collector’s edition, check out the original article here.

When Is The Official Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Showcase?

Leaks are all well and good, but if you’re hoping to see the real thing for yourself, you won’t have to wait much longer.

To find out when the Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Showcase is, check out the original article here.

Now that that’s all said and done, will you be picking up Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced when it releases this Summer? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming guides, check out the following:

  • All Xbox Game Pass Prices Following Game Pass Ultimate Price Drop, Explained

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Vitality Announces Counter-Strike Academy; Shares First Roster
item.content:encoded:
Team Vitality has announced the establishment of its Counter-Strike academy, Vitality Academy. The Vitality becomes the latest Counter-Strike organization to establish an academy, after several orgs have had success with their academy rosters.

Vitality Academy looks to retain Vitality’s advantage in the scene

The announcement of the academy roster brings Vitality in line with other high-profile organizations, such as NAVI, MOUZ, or Team Spirit, in fielding academy rosters.

Notable academy graduates currently active in Tier 1 include Drin ‘makazze’ Shaqiri (NAVI Jr), Ilya ‘m0NESY’ Osipov (NAVI Jr), Dorian ‘xertioN’ Berman (MOUZ), Danil ‘donk’ Kryshkovets (Spirit Academy), and Maksim ‘kyousuke’ Lukin, amongst many others.

The above examples have displayed that academies can be valuable to organizations both for direct promotions to the main team (makazze, donk, xertioN), and as a revenue driver when academy players are sold (kyousuke, m0nesy).

Therefore, academies can both generate revenue directly, through sales, and indirectly by saving money that would have otherwise been spent on transfers.

That aspect is especially relevant in 2026, with high player prices generally leading to roster stagnation in Tier 1.

While Vitality will be in no rush to make roster changes, the academy sets up a potential space for players to develop with Vitality’s systems and protocols ingrained into young players.

Vitality seemingly has the formula for success in Counter-Strike 2, with the main roster enjoying a period of unprecedented dominance.

The task for the academy will be to retain that advantage through embedding strong principles in its youngsters. That seems possible, with the academy and the main roster set to practice together and share feedback sessions.

The academy also enjoys a link to the main team through their coach, Pablo ‘VdaK1NG’ Escobar, who previously acted as an analyst for the main roster.

The Team Vitality Academy roster is:

Ulysse ‘katkame’ Thiebault (France, 16)
Luca-Adrian ‘lucaZ’ Gavrilut (France, 17)
Aleks ‘Reqqen’ Frolov (Estonia, 16)
Kirill ‘Dafra1D’ Polieiko (Ukraine, 19)
Patrick ‘patrenzo’ Hauer (Slovakia, 17)

Pablo ‘VdaK1NG’ Escobar (Coach)

Fabien ‘Neo’ Devide, President of Team Vitality, reflected on the benefit of the academy roster to the org, stating:

“Creating a Counter-Strike academy means investing in the future of our ecosystem. For esports to continue growing and structuring itself, it must rely on strong development pathways capable of identifying, supporting, and nurturing tomorrow’s talents.

“At Team Vitality, we want to perform today, but also build the champions of tomorrow and help elevate the entire scene to the next level.”

Vitality’s history with academy rosters

The org now fields academy rosters in Counter-Strike and League of Legends, where the Vitality academy competes in the LFL as Vitality.Bee.

Several of Vitality’s LoL academy players have gone on to compete in Tier 1, including current Vitality players Kadir ‘Fleshy’ Kemiksiz and Kaan ‘Naak Nako’ Okan.

The organization also briefly fielded a Valorant academy roster branded VIT.Revolution, but the roster disbanded after the conclusion of VCL France Stage 2 in May of last year.

Despite the brief existence of the roster, Vitality did successfully graduate one of its players to the main roster, with Ștefan ‘Sayonara’ Mîtcu taking his place in the org’s main Valorant roster.

Are you excited about Vitality’s roster? Discuss that on our Discord!
For more CS news, see our BLAST Rivals For Worth tournament guide.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Tarkov Players Find Mysterious ‘TarCoin’ Item Following Update
item.content:encoded:
Recently, Battlestate Games deployed the 1.0.4.5 update to Escape from Tarkov, implementing several balancing changes and introducing a few more optimizations to the popular extraction shooter.

However, users have noticed that not every change has made its way into the patch notes for 1.0.4.5. For instance, some avid Tarkov fans have taken to social media to report finding a mysterious new barter item called ‘TarCoin’ that was added during the recent update but was never formally addressed by Battlestate Games.

What is a TarCoin in Escape from Tarkov?

On Reddit and X, users started uploading images of a new loot item called a TarCoin. It’s an all-new valuable item in Escape from Tarkov, but at the time of writing, its use case was unknown. It’s yet to be picked up by sites like Tarkov Market.

The item’s in-game description reads:

A mysterious currency that appeared in Tarkov after the Contract Wars had already begun. The motto stamped on the coin refers to a certain well-known company… A rare item, prized by everyone without exception.

It’s a small, diamond-shaped token that cannot be sold on the Flea Market and cannot be discarded in raid. Aside from that, we know nothing.

On various forums, users surmised it’s something to do with the increasing connection between Escape from Tarkov and Escape from Tarkov: Arena, the session-based multiplayer spin-off. The recent event in Tarkov, dubbed Casus Belli, was all about that connection and fusing the two games further.

So far, users have reported finding the TarCoin in Escape from Tarkov in the biggest chalet on Lighthouse, and in Dorms Marked, but it’s safe to assume it could pop up anywhere.

Hopefully, we’ll learn more about this mysterious item soon.

Have you found the TarCoin in Escape from Tarkov? Let us know what you think about this item on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that the words ‘New Vegas 2’ were uttered by a former Fallout writer

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: ‘New Vegas 2’ Uttered by Fallout Writer, Igniting Hope
item.content:encoded:
In 2010, Obsidian and Bethesda pushed to market a game that remains a popular cult classic today. That was, of course, Fallout: New Vegas, widely considered by many to be the best Fallout game ever made. In case you’ve been living under some irradiated rock, New Vegas has been back in the news of late because of rumors surrounding a remaster or even a sequel.

In a recent interview, Chris Avellone, former Obsidian veteran and a writer of some of the most memorable Fallout sequences ever made, mentioned the words ‘New Vegas 2’, whipping the community into a frenzy.

New Vegas 2 Won’t Be a Thing

Chris Avellone appeared in a recent interview that lasted several hours. During that interview, he talked extensively about Van Buren, the cancelled Fallout project that would have been the real Fallout 3. These are topics that I spoke to Avellone about during a recent episode of Access Granted.

He explained how some ideas around the Van Buren concept were rehashed into Fallout: New Vegas in 2010, years after the ‘original Fallout 3’ project was shut down. For instance, the events of the Lonesome Road DLC would have eventually pushed into Van Buren’s story.

That’s why all those nukes go off, because I’m like, ‘well, I need to destabilize some regions.’

Not wipe out factions, but I need to shake up the Brotherhood, I need to shake up the NCR, I need to have them even more at each other’s throats in California.

Avellone then dropped a bombshell (pun kind of intended), stating that Fallout: New Vegas 2 would have expanded the Van Buren content that never saw the light of day. With Van Buren having died a death, New Vegas 2 would have been an outlet for that scrapped content, pushing into a realm not yet explored properly by Bethesda Game Studios.

Note: The third season of the Fallout television series might be going that way.

Per Avellone’s comments:

That was the reason for some of the pre-cursor decisions for some of the DLC stuff and some of the stuff in core New Vegas. When we still thought we’d be able to do a New Vegas 2, or whatever the title would be, but that quickly evaporated.

Unfortunately, the legendary writer and designer then shut down hopes by stating his clear belief about another New Vegas title surfacing: ‘It will not happen in the next six years at least, if ever.’

Would you like to see a Fallout: New Vegas 2? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Xbox Game Pass is getting a price cut

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1046)—Answers for Wednesday, April 22
item.content:encoded:
It’s midweek madness in the NYT Connections world, and you’re here for the best hints and answers you’ll find anywhere online! That’s fine, I’m more than happy to oblige. Below, you’ll find today’s NYT Connections solutions, along with some handy clues that will help you to solve today’s puzzle before you get the ultimate answers locked in.

Scroll down to get the NYT Connections hints you need for today’s challenge!

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (April 17)

Here are the words from today’s NYT Connections puzzle:

  • TRUCK
  • POLISH
  • NICE
  • SLUG
  • DECK
  • GAME
  • WHEEL
  • HERB
  • CLAY
  • SOCK
  • READING
  • ARTIST
  • STICKS
  • GLAZE
  • PUNCH
  • KILN

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Words you’d associate with pottery making
  • Green – Words you’d use in place of ‘smack’
  • Blue – Words you can pronounce differently depending on their use case
  • Purple – Words you’d see following ‘pick-up’

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, April 22:

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, April 22 (#1046)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – CLAY, GLAZE, KILN, WHEEL
  • Green – DECK, PUNCH, SLUG, SOCK
  • Blue – HERB, NICE, POLISH, READING
  • Purple – ARTIST, GAME, STICKS, TRUCK

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-connections-hints-solutions-april-22-1046/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Neverness to Everness Pre-Download Guide—How to Preload & File Sizes Explained
item.content:encoded:
Big releases continue to come thick and fast, and for those whose wallets have been stretched thin from the major releases, Neverness to Everness offers a rest bite, and a preload for the game will be available.

Neverness to Everness is a free-to-play Gacha game releasing on April 29, bringing GTA Online-style experiences, including housing, racing, and more.

If you’re eager to dive into Neverness to Everness as soon as the title is available, Insider Gaming has all the details on preloads.

How to Preload Neverness to Everness

Neverness to Everness will be available to preload up to two days before the official launch date, giving you time to ensure you’re fully prepared for the release.

Preloads for Neverness to Everness will be available from April 27, ahead of the April 29 release date, with players on PC and PlayStation 5 both able to preload the game ready for launch.

  • More Insider Gaming: NTE: Neverness to Everness Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times

It’s advisable to preload the game for launch due to the vast attention the title has received, garnering over 35 million pre-registrations, which means the servers could be overloaded when the game first drops.

That means that downloads of the full game could take longer to complete when the doors have opened, so it’s best to be prepared and get Neverness to Everness ready in advance.

Neverness to Everness File Sizes, Explained

Full file sizes of Neverness to Everness across all platforms have not yet been revealed, but the previous beta tests provide an indication of the size of the full game.

For the beta, the PlayStation 5 version of Neverness to Everness was at 54.42 GB, the largest file size of all, compared to around 35 GB on mobile devices.

  • More Insider Gaming: Is NTE: Neverness to Everness on PlayStation, Xbox & PC? All Platforms, Explained

Meanwhile, mobile players will need around 16 GB of storage space for Neverness to Everness, based on the previous beta sizes.

Of course, the final file sizes could be different from the beta, and this guide will be updated with the official file sizes when the exact details are known.

Are you planning to preload Neverness to Everness? Share your excitement for the game in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next read, find out about all the Neverness to Everness multiplayer details, and check out all the active Escape From Tarkov codes.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For April 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, April 22, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Used to store liquids
  • Hint 2: Often used by hikers or soldiers
  • Hint 3: Can also be used to reference a cafeteria

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Drink
  • Pint
  • Bag
  • Mug
  • Jug
  • Pouch
  • Thermos
  • Bottle
  • Flask

Today’s Contexto Answer For April 22, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Canteen

A Canteen is a small flask for carrying liquids, though it’s also used in reference to a dining facility similar to a cafeteria.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on April 23.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more word game guides, check out the following:

  • Wordle

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Does NTE: Neverness to Everness Have Multiplayer Or Co-Op?
item.content:encoded:
Major games continue to be released thick and fast in 2026, and plenty of eyes are turning towards Neverness to Everness, and we’ve got all the multiplayer details.

Neverness to Everness is a free-to-play, GTA Online-style Gacha title releasing on April 29, and has harnessed a wealth of attention by securing over 35 million pre-registrations.

If Neverness to Everness is on your radar and you want to know about the multiplayer and co-op options in the game, Insider Gaming has you covered.

Does Neverness to Everness Have Multiplayer?

Yes, Neverness to Everness offers multiplayer features, though the full details of exactly what is available remain to be seen.

Several updates from the developers have mentioned multiplayer features, with one of the biggest being online races against players across the world.

  • More Insider Gaming: Is NTE: Neverness to Everness on PlayStation, Xbox & PC? All Platforms, Explained

Neverness to Everness also features housing systems with online multiplayer modes, allowing players to visit their friends’ homes and check out their living spaces, similar to the features offered in GTA Online.

Online play could also include specific missions and player-vs-player content, but we’ll have to wait until the game’s launch to find out everything that’s under the hood.

Does Neverness to Everness Have Co-Op?

Neverness to Everness’ co-op features have not been fully revealed, though the multiplayer functionality is expected to include some co-op play.

Whether this extends to the main missions and the various jobs you can undertake remains to be seen, and full details won’t be known until closer to launch.

  • More Insider Gaming: NTE: Neverness to Everness Release Date Countdown & Exact Start Times

Releasing the game without any co-op would be a major oversight, though, so we expect there to be a large amount of content you can play with your friends.

Some details on the PlayStation store show that multiplayer of up to four can participate in co-op, with each bringing a squad of up to four characters into the fold.

When more details are available, this guide will be promptly updated.

What are you most excited for in Neverness to Everness? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next read, take a look at the games leaving Xbox Game Pass this month, and find out the available platforms for Invincible Vs.
